title: Atualização do Conflito Índia-Paquistão — 12 Abr 2026

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: Pakistan's facilitation of the US-Iran ceasefire (April 7) — validated by Trump's 'real agreement' announcement on April 9 — delivered a major diplomatic boost to Islamabad and forced a strategic reassessment in New Delhi. Field Marshal Asim Munir chaired the 274th Corps Commanders' Conference (April 8), condemning attacks on Saudi Arabia and reviewing regional security. Indian security forces killed two militants in Operation Trashi-1 in Kishtwar (April 8) and dismantled a major Srinagar-based LeT module, arresting five including two Pakistani nationals — one of whom had evaded capture for 16 years. Pakistan's Navy Chief met Turkish military leadership on April 7 to deepen bilateral defense cooperation. The tracker daycount passes 28,728 days of the India-Pakistan rivalry, with the LOC ceasefire holding despite sustained rhetorical escalation.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: Field Marshal Asim Munir chaired the 274th Corps Commanders' Conference (CCC) at GHQ Rawalpindi on April 8, 2026. J&K Police Bust Major Lashkar-e-Taiba Module in Srinagar — Key Operative Caught After 16 Years on the Run. Indian security forces killed two militants in an encounter in Kishtwar district, Jammu & Kashmir, as part of Operation Trashi-1.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: Pakistan Naval Chief and Turkish Military Agree to Strengthen Bilateral Military Cooperation. Pakistan announced on April 7, 2026 that it had facilitated a temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran, representing a significant diplomatic achievement for Islamabad.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Pakistani Defence Minister Khawaja Asif sparked an international incident by threatening to 'take it to Kolkata' if India stages a false flag operation, prompting West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee to demand Modi's resignation over the threat. India's Rajnath Singh had already warned (April 2) that Operation Sindoor 'is not over' and any Pakistani misadventure would draw an unprecedented response. Iran rejected Pakistan's mediation bid for the US-Iran conflict, undermining Islamabad's bid to reposition itself as a regional stabilizer. India passed the 100-day mark without responding to a UN deadline on the Indus Waters Treaty suspension, maintaining its position that the treaty remains in abeyance until Pakistan ends terror support. Security forces killed 13 'Indian-sponsored' militants in a KPK intelligence-based operation, as Pakistan continued its narrative linking internal instability to Indian interference.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Mamata Banerjee Demands Modi Response to Pakistan's Kolkata Threat; Diplomatic Row Intensifies. Pakistan's Leghari: India Cannot Unilaterally Suspend Indus Waters Treaty; Arbitration Obligatory.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Pakistan Defence Minister Khawaja Asif Threatens to 'Take It to Kolkata' if India Stages False Flag.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: An April 3, 2026 Foreign Policy analysis assessed Pakistan's diplomatic gambit of positioning itself as a mediator in the US-Iran conflict as a significant setback for India. India Passes 100-Day Mark Without Responding to UN Indus Waters Treaty Deadline.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: India's Shahpur Kandi Dam reached its March 31 target operational date, formally diverting surplus Ravi River waters from Pakistan — a key milestone in India's post-IWT water strategy. J&K CM Omar Abdullah announced plans to triple the UT's hydropower capacity from 3,540 MW to 11,000 MW by 2035, with NHPC and JKSPDCL signing a 500 MW expansion deal for Uri-I Stage-II and Dulhasti Stage-II projects. A British security think tank (BASIC) assessed the May 2025 India-Pakistan military crisis as the most severe confrontation since 1971, warning of dangerously low nuclear thresholds. Security forces conducted Operation Arahama in Ganderbal, J&K, killing at least one militant — the latest in a series of counter-insurgency operations. Meanwhile, only one LOC ceasefire violation has been recorded in all of 2026, reflecting the continued durability of the February 2021 DGMO agreement despite diplomatic tensions.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: Indian security forces launched Operation Arahama in the Arhama area of Ganderbal district, Jammu & Kashmir, on the night of March 31–April 1, 2026, killing at least one militant in a joint encounter.

title: India-Pakistan Conflict Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: India's Shahpur Kandi Dam nears operational status, set to divert Ravi River flows from Pakistan as part of the ongoing Indus Waters Treaty suspension — prompting President Zardari to demand immediate IWT reinstatement. A diplomatic row erupted after EAM Jaishankar called Pakistan a 'dalaal nation' at an all-party parliamentary meeting, dismissing its role as US-Iran mediator; Pakistan's FO responded with sharp rebukes. The US ODNI 2026 Annual Threat Assessment flagged Pakistan's ballistic missile program as a developing homeland threat and cited 'India-Pakistan relations as a risk for nuclear conflict.' NIA courts sentenced Kashmiri separatist Asiya Andrabi to life imprisonment under UAPA, drawing Pakistani condemnation and UN appeals.
